name: psytican-bot
documentation:
# Psytican chat helper bot

This bot can help you to book an event, list upcoming events and edit or delete your booking.

functions.name: src.telegram.utils.separate_callback_data
documentation: Separate the callback data

classes.name: src.telegram.redispesistence.RedisPersistence

methods.name: src.telegram.redispesistence.RedisPersistence.get_user_data
documentation: Returns the user_data from the pickle on Redis if it exists or an empty {obj}`defaultdict`.

methods.name: src.telegram.redispesistence.RedisPersistence.get_chat_data
documentation: Returns the chat_data from the pickle on Redis if it exists or an empty {obj}`defaultdict`.

methods.name: src.telegram.redispesistence.RedisPersistence.get_bot_data
documentation: Returns the bot_data from the pickle on Redis if it exists or an empty {obj}`dict`.

methods.name: src.telegram.redispesistence.RedisPersistence.get_conversations
documentation: Returns the conversations from the pickle on Redis if it exsists or an empty dict.

methods.name: src.telegram.redispesistence.RedisPersistence.update_conversation
documentation:
Will update the conversations for the given handler and depending
on {attr}`on_flush` save the pickle on Redis.

methods.name: src.telegram.redispesistence.RedisPersistence.update_user_data
documentation: Will update the user_data and depending on {attr}`on_flush` save the pickle on Redis.

methods.name: src.telegram.redispesistence.RedisPersistence.update_chat_data
documentation: Will update the chat_data and depending on {attr}`on_flush` save the pickle on Redis.

methods.name: src.telegram.redispesistence.RedisPersistence.update_bot_data
documentation: Will update the bot_data and depending on {attr}`on_flush` save the pickle on Redis.

methods.name: src.telegram.redispesistence.RedisPersistence.flush
documentation: Will save all data in memory to pickle on Redis.

methods.name: src.telegram.redispesistence.RedisPersistence.drop_chat_data
documentation:
Will delete the specified key from the `chat_data` and save the pickle file.
Args:

> chat_id ({obj}`int`): The chat id to delete from the persistence.

methods.name: src.telegram.redispesistence.RedisPersistence.drop_user_data
documentation:
Will delete the specified key from the `user_data` and save the pickle on Redis.
Args:

> user_id ({obj}`int`): The user id to delete from the persistence.

methods.name: src.telegram.redispesistence.RedisPersistence.get_callback_data
documentation:
Returns the callback data from the pickle file if it exists or {obj}`None`.

Returns:

: Tuple\[List\[Tuple\[{obj}`str`, {obj}`float`, Dict\[{obj}`str`, {class}`object`\]\]\],
  Dict\[{obj}`str`, {obj}`str`\]\] | {obj}`None`: The restored metadata or {obj}`None`,
  if no data was stored.

methods.name: src.telegram.redispesistence.RedisPersistence.refresh_bot_data
documentation: Does nothing.

methods.name: src.telegram.redispesistence.RedisPersistence.refresh_chat_data
documentation: Does nothing.

methods.name: src.telegram.redispesistence.RedisPersistence.refresh_user_data
documentation: Does nothing.

methods.name: src.telegram.redispesistence.RedisPersistence.update_callback_data
documentation:
Will update the callback_data (if changed) and save the pickle on Redis.
Args:

> data (Tuple\[List\[Tuple\[{obj}`str`, {obj}`float`, Dict\[{obj}`str`, {class}`object`\]\]\], Dict\[{obj}`str`, {obj}`str`\]\]):
>
> : The relevant data to restore {class}`telegram.ext.CallbackDataCache`.
Using Redis to make the bot persistent

functions.name: src.telegram.tg_calendar.create_callback_data
documentation: Create the callback data associated to each button

functions.name: src.telegram.tg_calendar.create_calendar
documentation:
Create an inline keyboard with the provided year and month
\:param int year: Year to use in the calendar, if None the current year is used.
\:param int month: Month to use in the calendar, if None the current month is used.
\:return: Returns the InlineKeyboardMarkup object with the calendar.

functions.name: src.telegram.tg_calendar.process_calendar_selection
documentation:
Process the callback_query. This method generates a new calendar if forward or
backward is pressed. This method should be called inside a CallbackQueryHandler.
\:param telegram.Bot bot: The bot, as provided by the CallbackQueryHandler
\:param telegram.Update update: The update, as provided by the CallbackQueryHandler
\:return: Returns a tuple (Boolean,datetime.datetime), indicating if a date is selected

> and returning the date if so.

functions.name: src.telegram.tg_wrapper.cancel
documentation: Cancels and ends the conversation.

functions.name: src.telegram.tg_wrapper.start
documentation: Sends a message with three inline buttons attached.

functions.name: src.telegram.tg_wrapper.help_command
documentation: Displays info on how to use the bot.

functions.name: src.telegram.tg_wrapper.button
documentation: Parses the CallbackQuery and updates the message text.

functions.name: src.telegram.tg_wrapper.ask_for_input
documentation: Prompt user to input data for selected feature.

functions.name: src.telegram.tg_wrapper.save_input
documentation: Save input for feature and return to feature selection.
